questions.question: What are the minimum system requirements to install and run Android Studio on a Windows machine?
keyPoints: At least 2 GB of available disk space (4 GB Recommended)

questions.question: Describe the purpose and installation process of the Android SDK (Software Development Kit) as part of the Android development environment setup.
keyPoints: The Android SDK provides the libraries, debugger, emulator, and other essential tools needed to build Android apps.
It is typically installed automatically through the Android Studio SDK Manager.
The SDK Manager allows developers to install specific platform versions (API levels) and system images for the emulator.

questions.question: A developer is encountering a 'SDK tools not found' error after a fresh Android Studio installation. Outline a comprehensive troubleshooting process.
keyPoints: Verify the SDK path in Android Studio (File > Project Structure > SDK Location)
Check if the ANDROID_HOME environment variable is set correctly
Use the SDK Manager to check if SDK packages are installed and re-install if necessary
Confirm required SDK tools folder exists within the SDK directory
